description: Storage Separado por Site - Isolar dados de demandas, concluídas e metadata por ambiente Fala.BR (produção, treinamento, homologação), mantendo config e preferências globais

userStories.title: Mapeamento de site ativo via URL
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a utility that identifies the active Fala.BR site from a URL, so the entire system knows which environment the data belongs to.
acceptanceCriteria: Create file shared/js/neuron-site.js with a NeuronSite IIFE (same pattern as NeuronDB)
NeuronSite.SITES constant maps aliases to domains: { producao: 'falabr.cgu.gov.br', treinamento: 'treinafalabr.cgu.gov.br', homologacao: 'falabr-h.cgu.gov.br' }
NeuronSite.getFromUrl(url) parses the hostname and returns the alias string ('producao', 'treinamento', or 'homologacao'), or null if unknown
NeuronSite.getDomain(alias) returns the full domain string for a given alias, or null if unknown
NeuronSite.getLabel(alias) returns short display labels: { producao: 'PROD', treinamento: 'TREINA', homologacao: 'HOMOLOG' }
Register neuron-site.js in manifest.json in ALL content_scripts blocks that include neuron-db.js — it must load BEFORE neuron-db.js
Also add neuron-site.js to the background.js importScripts call
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the foundation utility used by all other stories. Check manifest.json for all content_scripts entries that reference shared/js/neuron-db.js and add shared/js/neuron-site.js before it in each js array. Also add it to web_accessible_resources if neuron-db.js is listed there.

userStories.title: Chaves de storage com prefixo por site no NeuronDB
userStories.description: As a developer, I need storage keys for demandas, concluidas, and metadata to be prefixed with the site alias, so each environment has isolated data.
acceptanceCriteria: In neuron-db.js, the KEY_DEMANDAS, KEY_CONCLUIDAS, and KEY_METADATA constants are replaced with a function or getter that builds keys dynamically based on the active site alias (e.g., 'neuron_producao_demandas')
KEY_CONFIG ('neuron_config') and KEY_PREFERENCES ('neuron_preferences') remain unchanged (global, no prefix)
NeuronDB.init() accepts an optional siteAlias parameter — when provided, it sets the active site and builds per-site keys
When siteAlias is not provided to init(), it defaults to 'producao'
The in-memory cache loads data from the per-site keys for demandas/concluidas/metadata, and from global keys for config/preferences
All existing NeuronDB methods (saveDemanda, getAllDemandas, markConcluida, getMetadata, etc.) continue to work transparently using the per-site keys
The 'initialized' flag is reset when switching sites so init() re-reads from storage
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Core change to shared/js/neuron-db.js. Store the current siteAlias in a module-level variable. Build key names like `neuron_${siteAlias}_demandas`. The init() internal calls (await init()) in each method should not override the site — only the first explicit call sets it.

userStories.title: NeuronSync com suporte a chaves por site
userStories.description: As a developer, I need NeuronSync to maintain cache coherence for per-site prefixed storage keys, so changes in one context are reflected across all others.
acceptanceCriteria: NeuronSync's chrome.storage.onChanged listener detects changes to keys matching the pattern neuron_{alias}_{type} (e.g., neuron_producao_demandas)
When a per-site key changes, NeuronSync calls NeuronDB._updateCache only if the changed key belongs to the currently active site in NeuronDB
Add NeuronDB.getCurrentSiteAlias() or similar method so NeuronSync can check the active site
Config and preference listeners continue working unchanged (global keys, no prefix)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Modify shared/js/neuron-sync.js. The listener in ensureListener() needs to check if a changed key matches the current site before updating cache. Use NeuronDB._getCurrentSiteAlias() or expose it via the public API.

userStories.title: Inicialização do NeuronDB com contexto de site nos content scripts
userStories.description: As a content script, I need to initialize NeuronDB with the correct site detected from the page URL, so data is read/written to the correct namespace.
acceptanceCriteria: In modules/ouvidoria/tratar-novo/tratar-novo-insert.js, change NeuronDB.init() to NeuronDB.init(NeuronSite.getFromUrl(window.location.href))
In modules/loading/loading.js, if it calls NeuronDB.init(), update it similarly
In any other content script file that explicitly calls NeuronDB.init(), update to pass the site alias
Search all 17 files that reference NeuronDB for explicit init() calls and update them all
Data saved while on treinafalabr.cgu.gov.br is stored under neuron_treinamento_* keys
Data saved while on falabr.cgu.gov.br is stored under neuron_producao_* keys
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Search for 'NeuronDB.init()' across all files. Most NeuronDB methods call init() internally, but only the first explicit init() sets the site. Files to check: tratar-novo-insert.js, loading.js, popup.js, dashboard.js, options.js, notificacoes.js, module-factory.js. For popup/dashboard/options (extension pages, not content scripts), use 'producao' as default for now — US-007/US-008 will add the site selector later.

userStories.title: Correção do href hardcoded na extração de demandas
userStories.description: As a user, I want extracted demand links to point to the correct domain of the site I'm browsing, so I'm not redirected to production when I'm on training.
acceptanceCriteria: In modules/ouvidoria/tratar-novo/tratar-novo-extract.js line 57, replace the hardcoded 'https://falabr.cgu.gov.br' with window.location.origin
Demands extracted on treinafalabr.cgu.gov.br have href starting with https://treinafalabr.cgu.gov.br
Demands extracted on falabr-h.cgu.gov.br have href starting with https://falabr-h.cgu.gov.br
Demands extracted on falabr.cgu.gov.br continue to have href starting with https://falabr.cgu.gov.br
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Simple one-line change in tratar-novo-extract.js. Change: href: urlRelativo ? `https://falabr.cgu.gov.br${urlRelativo}` : null → href: urlRelativo ? `${window.location.origin}${urlRelativo}` : null

userStories.title: Descarte de dados antigos na atualização da extensão
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to clean up old-format storage data (without site prefix) when the extension is updated, to avoid orphaned or mixed data.
acceptanceCriteria: In background.js onInstalled listener, check if reason is 'install' or 'update'
Check if old-format keys exist: 'neuron_demandas', 'neuron_concluidas', 'neuron_metadata'
If they exist, remove them using chrome.storage.local.remove(['neuron_demandas', 'neuron_concluidas', 'neuron_metadata'])
Do NOT remove neuron_config or neuron_preferences (these are still global)
Set a flag 'neuron_storage_v2' in chrome.storage.local so this cleanup only runs once
Log a console message when cleanup is performed
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Modify background.js. Add cleanup logic inside the existing chrome.runtime.onInstalled listener after the config initialization. Check for the v2 flag first to avoid re-running.

userStories.title: NeuronDB.switchSite para troca de contexto
userStories.description: As a popup/dashboard, I need to query data from any site (not just the active one), to show data from the site chosen by the user.
acceptanceCriteria: Add NeuronDB.switchSite(siteAlias) method that changes the active site and reloads the cache from storage
switchSite resets the 'initialized' flag and calls init(siteAlias) internally
Add NeuronDB.getCurrentSite() method that returns the current site alias string
After switchSite, all read/write operations (getAllDemandas, saveDemanda, etc.) use the new site's keys
Config and preferences remain global regardless of which site is active
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Modify shared/js/neuron-db.js. switchSite should set initialized=false, update the siteAlias variable, then call init(). Expose getCurrentSite in the public API return object.

userStories.title: Seletor de site no Popup
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see which site's data the popup is showing and switch to another site, to compare demands between environments.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a segmented control (3 buttons side by side) in the popup header with labels PROD, TREINA, HOMOLOG
On popup open, detect the active tab's URL using chrome.tabs.query({active: true, currentWindow: true}) and auto-select the matching site
If the active tab is not a Fala.BR site, default to PROD
Clicking a different site button calls NeuronDB.switchSite() and refreshes all popup data (counters, notifications)
Color coding: PROD=blue (#2563eb), TREINA=orange (#ea580c), HOMOLOG=green (#16a34a) — active button gets the color, inactive buttons are gray
The segmented control is placed below the master switch and above the notification settings
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Modify modules/popup/popup.html and modules/popup/popup.js. Use chrome.tabs.query to detect the active URL, then NeuronSite.getFromUrl() to get the alias. Style the segmented control to match the existing popup design. Ensure NeuronSite is available — check that popup.html loads neuron-site.js.

userStories.title: Seletor de site no Dashboard
userStories.description: As a user, I want to switch between sites on the Dashboard to view statistics for each environment separately.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a segmented control in the Dashboard header (same visual pattern as popup: PROD, TREINA, HOMOLOG)
On dashboard open, detect the site from the opener tab or default to PROD
Clicking a different site button calls NeuronDB.switchSite() and refreshes all charts, demand table, and counters
Color coding matches the popup: PROD=blue, TREINA=orange, HOMOLOG=green
The segmented control is placed in the dashboard header area, near the title
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Modify modules/dashboard/dashboard.html and modules/dashboard/dashboard.js. The dashboard is opened as an options page or new tab. It may not have a direct reference to the active Fala.BR tab, so default to 'producao' or check via chrome.tabs.query. Ensure neuron-site.js is loaded in dashboard.html. Reuse the same segmented control CSS from the popup story.
